full_name: Calcium Voltage-Gated Channel Subunit Alpha1 C

source.title: Altered function of lateral orbitofrontal cortex during reward processing in bipolar mania

AA.effect: AA carriers have altered L-type calcium channel function with reduced CACNA1C expression in the brain. Associated with approximately 1.2-fold increased bipolar disorder risk. AA individuals may show altered response to lithium therapy with potential for reduced therapeutic efficacy. Neurobiological changes affect emotional processing and reward circuitry.

AG.effect: AG carriers show intermediate CACNA1C expression with moderate changes in calcium channel function. Associated with mild increase in bipolar disorder risk and intermediate lithium response. Moderately altered emotional and reward processing.

GA.effect: GA carriers show intermediate CACNA1C expression with moderate changes in calcium channel function. Associated with mild increase in bipolar disorder risk and intermediate lithium response. Moderately altered emotional and reward processing.

GG.effect: GG carriers have normal L-type calcium channel function and standard CACNA1C expression in brain. Baseline bipolar disorder risk with typical mood stabilizer response patterns. Normal emotional and reward processing.

notes: CACNA1C rs1006737 is one of the most consistently replicated genetic findings in bipolar disorder GWAS studies. The risk allele (A) functions as a cis-expression QTL, reducing CACNA1C expression and affecting calcium signaling critical for synaptic plasticity and mood regulation.
